name: Hong Kong
plain_words: Profits tax 8.25% on first HK$2M, 16.5% above. Salaries tax progressive 2%-17% or standard 15%, whichever is lower. NO GST or VAT. Simple by design.

taxes.name: Profits Tax
taxes.plain_words: Tax on company profits. Two-tiered: 8.25% on first HK$2 million, 16.5% on the rest. File within 1 month of issue of Profits Tax Return.
taxes.notes: Two-tiered rates system since 2018. First HK$2M at 8.25%, above at 16.5%.

deadlines.name: profits_tax_return
deadlines.detail: File within 1 month of return issue

taxes.name: Salaries Tax
taxes.plain_words: Tax on employment income. Progressive 2%-17% OR standard rate 15%, whichever is lower. Basic allowance HK$132,000. No GST. Simple by design.
taxes.notes: Two methods: progressive (2/7/12/17%) or standard (15%). IRD charges the LOWER amount. Basic allowance HK$132,000. Married person allowance HK$264,000.

rates.name: standard_rate
rates.notes: Applied to net income after allowances if lower than progressive

deadlines.name: salaries_tax_return
deadlines.detail: File within 1 month of return issue (BIR60 form)
deadlines.penalty: Estimated assessment + surcharge up to 3x tax

taxes.name: Property Tax
taxes.plain_words: Tax on rental income from real estate. Rate 15% on net assessable value (rental income minus 20% for repairs).
taxes.notes: Owner-occupied properties exempt. Only applies to rental income.

rates.name: standard
rates.notes: On net assessable value (rent minus 20% allowance)

deadlines.name: property_tax_return
deadlines.detail: File within 1 month of return issue

taxes.name: No GST/VAT
taxes.plain_words: Hong Kong does NOT levy a general consumption tax. No GST. No VAT. This IS the simplicity. Not everything needs a sales tax.
taxes.notes: Hong Kong has deliberately chosen not to introduce GST/VAT. The government has considered it multiple times but decided against it, citing simplicity and competitiveness.
